mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals tightening labor market and rising wage pressures, which compress corporate profit margins and reduce equity valuations. This mechanism produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as markets price in margin compression.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.1% as of August 2014 reflects ongoing labor market recovery
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with tightening labor slack
failure_condition: Rank IC between UNRATE momentum and 20-day forward returns exceeds 0.05 in absolute value, or negative returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE declines by >0.2pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal strengthening residential investment and consumer confidence, which drives aggregate demand and corporate earnings growth. This mechanism produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1093.0 thousand units in July 2014 shows continued housing recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 52% of instances when HOUST rises >5% over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains near zero while inflation (CPIAUCSL) begins to accelerate, real rates turn negative and equity risk premiums compress. This interaction produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities as inflation hedges.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% and CPIAUCSL at 237.909 as of decision date
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy with emerging inflation
failure_condition: Interaction term (FEDFUNDS × CPIAUCSL momentum) produces Rank IC < 0.06 over any 18-month rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 58% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing activity and capacity utilization, which raises corporate profitability and equity valuations. This mechanism produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets react to supply-side strength.
regime_hint: economic expansion with rising capacity utilization
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 54% of instances when INDPRO accelerates >0.3% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) combined with rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals robust consumer spending and labor income, which drives corporate revenue growth and equity returns. This interaction produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days when both series accelerate simultaneously.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 444377.0 million and PAYEMS at 139118.0 thousand in August 2014
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion with labor market strength
failure_condition: Interaction term (RSAFS growth × PAYEMS growth) produces Rank IC < 0.07 over any 18-month rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held constant while real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 2% annualized, monetary policy becomes relatively restrictive. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as markets anticipate policy error and earnings downgrades.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0% AND FEDFUNDS unchanged for >2 months
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 15994.3 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q2 2014
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown with unchanged monetary stance
failure_condition: Interaction term produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 18-month rolling window, or negative returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and equity valuations. This mechanism produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as markets react to labor market strength.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 139118.0 thousand in August 2014 reflects ongoing job creation
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS momentum and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 53% of instances when PAYEMS rises >150k monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production combined with rising unemployment signals supply-side weakness and demand destruction, which compresses corporate margins and equity valuations. This interaction produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as markets price in stagflationary risks.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.1152 and UNRATE at 6.1% as of August 2014
regime_hint: supply shock with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: Interaction term (INDPRO decline × UNRATE rise) produces Rank IC < 0.06 over any 18-month rolling window, or negative returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances.
